title: An item being labeled as one thing and living in multiple groups of the same board
excerpt: Hello, we are using monday.com to track miniscule jira point items. Some items need to categorized with multiple labels and live in different groups on the same board. Each item is only allowed one label unless the dropd…

posts.author: as.affiliated (AS)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, we are using monday.com to track miniscule jira point items. Some items need to categorized with multiple labels and live in different groups on the same board. Each item is only allowed one label unless the dropdown column is used. Is there a way for an item to live in multiple groups on the same board?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @as.affiliated, there really isn’t a standard way to accomplish this in monday.

One option would be to use a connect board column, connected to the same board. This way you could mirror columns from the “main” item to the other items however depending on how many columns you need to see that might not be overly efficient.

The other option would be to create a make.com scenario to manage this, however there would need to be some wat to identify like items to keep in sync.

Hope this helps!
Mark

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @as.affiliated

I could help you with this use case.

If you’re also looking to have the same item exist on two different boards but with different names, this app of ours could help - Mirror Item Multiple Boards lets you:

  • Keep one item synced across unlimited boards (even with different names)
  • Update anywhere, changes reflect everywhere instantly
  • Add subitems, comments, and files in the sync
  • Exclude certain columns
  • See live sync status in using the sync table feature

How it works: You just select an item, choose which boards to mirror it to, and that’s it. Any updates sync automatically in real-time.
